title: Asynchronous Architecture Decisions for Go Services · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release board compares near-miss Go and RabbitMQ decisions for asynchronous architecture decisions for go services and accepts only bounded ownership, causal evidence, and an explicit live-broker transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Go service coupling and broker-fit decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose asynchronous messaging only when temporal decoupling, buffering, fan-out, failure isolation, or replay creates measurable value; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Distinguish competing consumers on one queue from publish/subscribe using one queue per subscriber responsibility; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evaluate request/reply over messaging against ordinary HTTP using correlation, timeout, cancellation, failure ambiguity, and capacity evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Temporal Coupling Decision · changed case · Quiz Temporal Coupling Decision
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 5365 during canary Go consumer decoding old and new envelopes. Build deterministic Go evidence to choose HTTP, work distribution, pub/sub, or retained streams from coupling evidence; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then remove the parking binding and expose the remediation gap. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose asynchronous messaging only when temporal decoupling, buffering, fan-out, failure isolation, or replay creates measurable value; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Run the smallest deterministic RabbitMQ and Go messaging model and retain inspectable changed-case evidence for the quiz Temporal Coupling Decision case
Inject one changed RabbitMQ and Go failure, verify bounded cleanup, and state live-broker transfer limits for the quiz Temporal Coupling Decision case

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Broker Fit Boundary
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Broker Fit Boundary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 3032 during table-driven topology review for a tenant migration. Build deterministic Go evidence to choose HTTP, work distribution, pub/sub, or retained streams from coupling evidence; retain rollback topology before deleting old bindings; then remove the parking binding and expose the remediation gap. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare RabbitMQ queues, streams, databases, job schedulers, and logs against ordering, retention, replay, routing, throughput, and operational constraints; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Broker Fit Boundary.
steps.why: Retrieving Broker Fit Boundary through the concrete a Go service coupling and broker-fit decision record case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Broker Fit Boundary, keep Broker Fit Boundary connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare RabbitMQ queues, streams, databases, job schedulers, and logs against ordering, retention, replay, routing, throughput, and operational constraints; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Broker Fit Boundary, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Broker Fit Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Broker Fit Boundary, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A quiz Go messaging team handles case 3032 during table-driven topology review for a tenant migration. Build deterministic Go evidence to choose HTTP, work distribution, pub/sub, or retained streams from coupling evidence; retain rollback topology before deleting old bindings; then remove the parking binding and expose the remediation gap. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare RabbitMQ queues, streams, databases, job schedulers, and logs against ordering, retention, replay, routing, throughput, and operational constraints; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Broker Fit Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: RabbitMQ queues are a durable event log and should retain all history indefinitely. In Go, adding a channel or broker wrapper does not remove temporal coupling.
